title: Payment amount: String → u64 zatoshi in wire types
description:
## Why

PaymentAction::Request and PaymentAction::Sent carry amount as a String (e.g. "0.1"). This is inconsistent with PaymentSession.amount_zatoshi which is already u64. String amounts are a latent bug: floating-point string parsing is fragile, "0.10" and "0.1" are different strings that mean the same amount, and there is no enforced unit.

## What needs to change

In core/src/messages.rs, change the two wire variants:

```rust
// BEFORE
PaymentAction::Request { chain: Chain, amount: String }
PaymentAction::Sent { chain: Chain, tx_hash: String, amount: String }

// AFTER
PaymentAction::Request { chain: Chain, amount_zatoshi: u64 }
PaymentAction::Sent { chain: Chain, tx_hash: String, amount_zatoshi: u64 }
```

All call sites that construct or destructure these variants must be updated (grep for PaymentAction::Request and PaymentAction::Sent across the workspace).

Display formatting (showing "0.001 ZEC" in the TUI/CLI) should convert from zatoshi at the presentation layer only: amount_zatoshi as f64 / 1e8. Do not store or transmit as float.

## Scope

- core/src/messages.rs — change the enum variants
- cli/src/commands.rs — any amount parsing from user input (parse as decimal ZEC string, multiply by 1e8, truncate to u64)
- tui/ — same display conversion
- wallet/ — verify no double-conversion with existing zatoshi amounts
- Any integration tests that construct PaymentAction with a string amount

## Acceptance Criteria

- PaymentAction::Request.amount_zatoshi and PaymentAction::Sent.amount_zatoshi are u64
- No String amount field remains in PaymentAction variants
- User-facing input still accepts decimal ZEC strings ("0.001"); parsing happens at the CLI/TUI boundary only
- Unit test: "0.001" ZEC parses to 100_000 zatoshi; "1.0" parses to 100_000_000
- cargo test --workspace passes with no warnings
- Wire format change is a breaking change — note in commit message that old clients will not interop
close_reason: amount field changed to u64 zatoshi; Chain derives Copy; breaking change documented; all tests pass
